challenges.title: Heading Hierarchy
challenges.description: Create a page using <h1>, <h2>, and <h3> headings in proper hierarchy. The h1 should be the main title, h2 a section heading, and h3 a sub-section heading. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.test_code:
assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an

heading' assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should close the

heading' assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an

heading' assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should close the

heading' assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an

heading' assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should close the

heading' assert html.lower().index('

') < html.lower().index('

'), 'The

should appear before

' assert html.lower().index('

') < html.lower().index('

'), 'The

should appear before

'
hints: Headings go from <h1> (most important) to <h6> (least important)
A proper hierarchy means h2 comes after h1, and h3 comes after h2 — never skip levels

challenges.title: Article Headings
challenges.description: Create a page with an <h1> title, then two <h2> section headings, each followed by an <h3> sub-heading. This creates a proper document outline. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.test_code:
assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an

heading' assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should close the

heading' assert html.lower().count('

') >= 2, 'Should contain at least two

headings' assert html.lower().count('

') >= 2, 'Should close both

headings' assert html.lower().count('

') >= 2, 'Should contain at least two

sub-headings' assert html.lower().count('

') >= 2, 'Should close both

sub-headings' assert html.lower().index('

') < html.lower().index('

'), 'The

should appear before any

'
hints: Think of it like a table of contents: one main title, two sections, each with a subsection
Example structure: h1 > h2 > h3, then another h2 > h3
